element_introduction_order.element: Four-button attack system (Light Punch, Light Kick, Strong Punch, Strong Kick)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Basic combo foundation for all subsequent matches
Players learn plane-switching via simultaneous Light Punch + Light Kick input

element_introduction_order.element: Plane Move Attack (Low and High variants)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Cross-plane offensive techniques; punch button for low approach, kick for high

element_introduction_order.element: Power Attack (knock opponent to alternate plane)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Forced positioning tool; some stages have hazards on one plane
Environmental punishment mechanic; restricts plane-switching on certain stages
Defensive skill requiring successful guard timing

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Available from start but emphasized mid-game
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Optional psychological tool; no mechanical necessity
Only executable at 25% health (flashing red); high-risk turnaround mechanic

element_introduction_order.element: Boss-exclusive movesets (Billy Kane, Axel Hawk, Laurence Blood, Wolfgang Krauser)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: New attack patterns; no direct tutorial; pattern recognition required

typical_relief_triggers: Opponent knocked to hazard plane for damage
Completion of bonus round (points reset tension)
typical_tension_triggers: Opponent enters Desperation Move range (25% health, flashing)
Hazard plane active; risky plane-switching decisions
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating tournament structure: 8 playable character matches establish core mechanics and rhythm; every 4th match (bonus round) provides rhythm reset; final 4 boss matches introduce new movesets and higher damage output, creating sustained tension through pattern-learning requirement

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Bonus rounds appear after every 4th match; reward points but are not mandatory for progression

difficulty_curve.early_game: First 4 matches: core four-button system and basic plane-switching introduced against playable characters with standard movesets; moderate difficulty to establish player muscle memory
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Matches 5-8: hazard stages introduced (electrified wires, bull stampede); evasion attacks and power attacks emphasized; difficulty increases via environmental constraints rather than character complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Matches 9-12 (boss gauntlet): four non-playable boss characters with exclusive, aggressive movesets (Billy Kane bojutsu, Axel Hawk boxing, Laurence Blood bullfighting style, Wolfgang Krauser nobility fighting); boss patterns require active adaptation; Desperation Move window (25% health) becomes critical
notable_spikes: Match 5: First hazard stage appearance; restricts plane-switching strategy
Match 9: Billy Kane (first boss); bojutsu moveset diverges significantly from playable character patterns
Match 12: Wolfgang Krauser (final boss); described as searching for player character; implied to be highest difficulty spike

notable_level_patterns.description: Two-plane system fully exploitable; player has freedom to control plane-switching for spacing and approach angles; used for playable character matches and some boss encounters

One plane contains environmental damage (electrified wires or bull stampede); player cannot voluntarily switch to hazard plane but can force opponent there via Power Attack for guaranteed extra damage; alters spacing strategy and damage economy
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid-tournament matches (5-8); examples include Australia stage (bull stampede)

notable_level_patterns.description: Four sequential non-playable boss encounters with exclusive movesets; no formal stage hazards documented; emphasis shifts from mechanics mastery to opponent-specific adaptation; Desperation Move availability becomes tactical
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Matches 9-12 (Billy Kane, Axel Hawk, Laurence Blood, Wolfgang Krauser)

notable_level_patterns.description: Rhythm break between tournament blocks; point-accumulation minigame; resets psychological tension before next difficulty tier; optional progression (not required to advance)
